---
title: "ChatGPT Promts"
author: "Annick Robatel"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Here are the promts I used to write to ChatGPT when i got stuck

1.  Is "mep-project-research-4.5." an adequate agent-name to use for the European Parliment open AI?

2.  What exactly does fromJSON(data, flatten = TRUE) do?

3.  I want to save meps_df as csv in data raw now. What is the correct code?

4.  I don't have automatic columns for name and country only the names. Could I write a Loop that gets me directly all the women from the newest parlamentary term (10) and creating new columns for gender and country?

5.  Something does not work. I get Error: parse error: premature EOF (right here) ------\^

6.  I ran: all_women \<- list()\
    \
    for(country in countries){\
    \
    url \<- paste0(\
    "https://data.europarl.europa.eu/api/v2/meps?",\
    "parliamentary-term=10",\
    "&gender=FEMALE",\
    "&country-of-representation=", country,\
    "&limit=500"\
    )\
    \
    response \<- GET(\
    url,\
    add_headers(\
    "User-Agent" = "mep-project-research-4.5.0",\
    "Accept" = "application/ld+json"\
    )\
    )\
    \
    data \<- content(response, as = "text", encoding = "UTF-8")\
    data_json \<- jsonlite::fromJSON(data, flatten = TRUE)\
    \
    df \<- as.data.frame(data_json\$data)\
    \
    df\$country \<- country\
    \
    all_women\[\[country\]\] \<- df\
    }\
    \
    women_df \<- bind_rows(all_women), something still does not work. How could i get it to run?

7.  explain this code line to line to me

```         
if (status_code(response) != 200) next

txt \<- content(response, as = "text", encoding = "UTF-8")

if (nchar(txt) == 0) next

parsed \<- tryCatch( fromJSON(txt, flatten = TRUE), error = function(e) NULL )

if (is.null(parsed)) next if (is.null(parsed$data)) next
  if (length(parsed$data) == 0) next
```

8.  Can I do the same for the men now?

9.  what do i get now?

    for (country in countries) {

    url \<- paste0(

    "https://data.europarl.europa.eu/api/v2/meps?",

    "&gender=FEMALE",

    "&country-of-representation=", country,

    "&limit=500"

    )

    response \<- GET(

    url,

    add_headers(

    "User-Agent" = "mep-project-research-4.5.0",

    "Accept" = "application/ld+json"

    )

    )

    cat("Country:", country, "- Status:", status_code(response), "\\n")

    if (status_code(response) != 200) next

    txt \<- content(response, as = "text", encoding = "UTF-8")

    if (nchar(txt) == 0) next

    parsed \<- tryCatch(

    fromJSON(txt, flatten = TRUE),

    error = function(e) NULL

    )

    if (is.null(parsed)) next

    if (is.null(parsed\$data)) next

    if (length(parsed\$data) == 0) next

    df \<- as.data.frame(parsed\$data)

    if (nrow(df) == 0) next

    df\$country \<- country

    df\$gender \<- "FEMALE"

    all_women\[\[country\]\] \<- df

    }

    women_df \<- bind_rows(all_women)

    glimpse(women_df)

    write_csv(women_df, "data_raw/meps_women_allterms_by_country.csv")

10. How could I now get the political group in my loop as well?

11. Does it matter which loop is inside the bigger loop?

12. Am I missing brackets somewhere? for (country in countries) {\
    for (group in groups) {\
    \
    url \<- paste0(\
    "https://data.europarl.europa.eu/api/v2/meps?",\
    "parliamentary-term=10",\
    "&gender=FEMALE",\
    "&country-of-representation=", URLencode(country, reserved = TRUE),\
    "&political-group=", URLencode(group, reserved = TRUE),\
    "&limit=500"\
    )\
    \
    response \<- GET(\
    url,\
    add_headers(\
    "User-Agent" = "mep-project-research-4.5.0",\
    "Accept" = "application/ld+json"\
    )\
    )\
    \
    if (status_code(response) != 200) next\
    \
    txt \<- content(response, as = "text", encoding = "UTF-8")\
    if (nchar(txt) == 0) next\
    \
    parsed \<- tryCatch(\
    fromJSON(txt, flatten = TRUE),\
    error = function(e) NULL\
    )\
    \
    if (is.null(parsed) \|\| is.null(parsed\$data) \|\| length(parsed\$data) == 0) next\
    \
    df \<- as.data.frame(parsed\$data)\
    if (nrow(df) == 0) next\
    \
    df\$country \<- country\
    df\$political_group \<- group\
    df\$gender \<- "FEMALE"\
    df\$parliamentary_term \<- 10\
    \
    all_women\[\[paste(country, group, sep = "\_")\]\] \<- df\
    }\
    }

13. Why are in meps_women_term10_country_group only 36 women?

14. What does Status 204 mean?

15. Would it be better to get the terms seperately?

16. How

17. 

18. 

19. Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message. Why?

20. Can you now combine the loops in one big loop with country and group for females?

21. Explain the loop line by line to me.

22. for (term in terms) { for (country in countries) { for (group in groups) { -\> so it looks at terms first, in the term for country and in the country for group?

23. 

24. Error in curl::curl_fetch_memory(url, handle = handle) : Timeout was reached \[data.europarl.europa.eu\]: Operation too slow. Less than 1 bytes/sec transferred the last 600 seconds. What does that mean?

25. Now I need a loop to get the number of speeches per MEP. How could i count them directly and not only get the different speeches?

26. With that loop I got way too many infos. I only need the number of speeches per MEP and the parlamentary term.

27. person_id \<- meps_preprocessed\$identifier\[i\] do i nummerate the speeches like that?

28. There are only speeches for the 9 and 10 term... can you adjust the loop?

29. Can I then merge my new df to my existing one with the MEPS and their infos?

30. 

##
